criteria.label: Name 6+ patterns from the rubric in under 30 seconds without notes.
criteria.example: Two-pointer, sliding window, hash map, BFS, DFS, heap, binary search, sort+sweep, greedy, DP — out loud.

criteria.label: Use input bounds to predict the expected complexity class and pattern.
criteria.example: `n ≤ 18` → bitmask DP / brute. `n ≤ 5000` → O(n²) DP. `n ≤ 10⁵` → O(n log n) sort + sweep.

criteria.label: Drop the standard template (BFS skeleton, sliding-window skeleton, DP recurrence) before specializing.
Recognize blended patterns (sort + sliding window, prefix-sum + hash, BFS + memo) and compose them cleanly.
criteria.example: 'Sub-array sum equals K' on unsorted ints = prefix sums + hash of seen prefix sums.

criteria.label: Justify in 1 sentence why the chosen pattern is correct AND why simpler patterns don't apply.
criteria.example: 'Greedy by deadline works because picking the earliest deadline doesn't preclude any later valid choice (greedy-choice property).'
